prompt: Medicare Part A primarily helps pay for:

options.text: Inpatient hospital stays and skilled nursing facility care (limited)
Retail outpatient prescriptions always
Routine dental cleanings only
Auto insurance premiums

explanation: Part A is hospital insurance covering inpatient hospital, SNF, hospice, some home health.

distractorExplanations.b: Part D covers most retail drugs.

prompt: Medicare Part B generally covers:

options.text: Long-term custodial nursing home care only
Physician services, outpatient care, preventive services, and DME
Hearing aids in all cases fully
Veterinary services

explanation: Part B is medical insurance for outpatient professional services and equipment.

prompt: Initial Enrollment Period (IEP) for Medicare typically spans:

options.text: One day only on birthday
Anytime without penalty forever
7 months around 65th birthday (3 before, month of, 3 after)
Only during open enrollment Oct–Dec for Part B first time always

explanation: IEP allows Part A/B enrollment around age 65 with permanent penalties if delayed without qualifying coverage.

prompt: Medicare Part D covers:

options.text: Inpatient surgery room only
Medigap premiums automatically
Part A deductibles exclusively
Outpatient prescription drugs through private plans

explanation: Part D provides drug coverage via stand-alone PDP or MA-PD plans.

prompt: Medicare Advantage (Part C) is:

options.text: Alternative way to receive Part A and B benefits through approved private plans
Replacement of all Medicare rights permanently by law
Only for under age 65 disabled never
Identical to Medigap always

explanation: MA plans must cover Part A/B benefits, often with Part D and extra benefits.

distractorExplanations.d: Medigap supplements Original Medicare, not MA same way.

prompt: Medigap (Medicare Supplement) policies:

options.text: Include Part D drug coverage in all plans since 2006
Help pay cost-sharing in Original Medicare, standardized in most states
Are sold by Medicare directly only
Replace Part A hospital entirely

explanation: Medigap fills gaps like coinsurance/deductibles; separate Part D needed.

prompt: Part B late enrollment penalty applies when:

options.text: Individual enrolls during IEP with no gaps
Individual has employer group coverage qualifying delay
Individual without creditable coverage delays Part B enrollment
Individual only has Part A free always exempt from all penalties

explanation: Part B penalty increases premium 10% per full 12-month period delayed without creditable coverage.

distractorExplanations.b: Special Enrollment Period may apply with group coverage.
distractorExplanations.d: Part B may still need enrollment decision.

prompt: Creditable prescription drug coverage means:

options.text: Any discount card without actuarial value
No drug coverage ever creditable
Only VA coverage never creditable
Coverage expected to pay on average at least as much as standard Part D

explanation: Employer/plan must certify creditable coverage to avoid Part D late penalty.

distractorExplanations.c: VA may be creditable in some cases.

prompt: Medicare Open Enrollment Period (OEP) Jan 1–Mar 31 allows beneficiaries with Medicare Advantage to:

options.text: Switch MA plans or return to Original Medicare with Part D enrollment rules
Enroll in Part A first time at any age penalty-free always
Buy Medigap guaranteed issue in all states always without rules
Cancel Medicare entirely online instantly

explanation: MA OEP permits one change among MA or disenroll to Original Medicare.

distractorExplanations.b: Part A first enrollment uses IEP/SEP.
distractorExplanations.c: Medigap GI limited outside certain windows.

prompt: Annual Election Period (AEP) Oct 15–Dec 7 is used to:

options.text: Pay Part A premiums only
Change Part D, join/switch/drop MA, or return to Original Medicare effective Jan 1
Enroll in Medicaid automatically always
Change Social Security retirement age

explanation: AEP is primary annual window for Medicare Advantage and Part D changes.

prompt: Extra Help (LIS) program assists with:

options.text: Medigap Plan F sales only
Long-term care insurance exclusively
Part D premiums, deductibles, and copays for limited-income beneficiaries
Auto loan payments

explanation: Low-Income Subsidy reduces Part D costs for qualifying beneficiaries.

prompt: Medicare hospice benefit focuses on:

options.text: Curative chemotherapy always covered same as active treatment without election
Only inpatient hospital unlimited years
Dental implants fully covered
Comfort care for terminal illness with prognosis six months or less if disease runs normal course

explanation: Hospice emphasizes palliative care; curative treatment generally stops upon hospice election with exceptions.

distractorExplanations.a: Curative intent shifts with hospice election.
distractorExplanations.b: Hospice includes home and other settings.

prompt: Part A hospital benefit period deductible:

options.text: Applies per benefit period, not calendar year only
Never applies if patient has Medigap
Paid by Medicare always with no beneficiary share
Only for outpatient visits

explanation: Benefit period starts when admitted and ends after 60 days without inpatient care.

distractorExplanations.b: Medigap may cover deductible depending on plan.

prompt: Special Enrollment Period (SEP) for Part B may occur when:

options.text: Beneficiary wants different TV channel
Employer group health coverage ends
Any random month without reason
Neighbor suggests switching

explanation: Loss of creditable employer coverage triggers SEP to enroll Part B without penalty.

prompt: SHIP counselor explaining Original vs Medicare Advantage should emphasize:

options.text: One option always best for everyone
Counselor receives commission from plan chosen
Network, cost-sharing, drug coverage, travel, and provider access differences neutrally
Beneficiary must decide in 5 minutes without materials

explanation: Neutral counseling compares options using unbiased materials without sales pressure.

distractorExplanations.b: SHIP counselors do not sell or commission.

prompt: Medicare Summary Notice (MSN) for Original Medicare shows:

options.text: Part D drug formulary only always
Medigap commission rates
Counselor salary
Services billed, Medicare-approved amounts, and beneficiary liability

explanation: MSN is quarterly statement of Part A/B claims processed, not a bill itself usually.

distractorExplanations.a: Part D uses EOB from plan.

prompt: Beneficiary appeal rights for denied Medicare coverage include levels such as:

options.text: Redetermination, reconsideration, ALJ hearing, and further review
No appeal ever for any denial
Only social media complaint
Single phone call final always

explanation: Medicare appeals process has defined escalating levels with time limits.

prompt: Advance Beneficiary Notice of Noncoverage (ABN) purpose is to:

options.text: Guarantee Medicare payment
Inform beneficiary Medicare may not pay and obtain choice to receive service with potential liability
Replace Medicare card
Enroll in Part D automatically

explanation: ABN documents informed consent when provider believes service may not be covered.

prompt: Beneficiary privacy rights under HIPAA include:

options.text: Public posting of all diagnoses online by counselor
Mandatory sharing of SSN with marketers
Access to records, accounting of disclosures, and complaint rights
No restrictions on counselor note storage ever

explanation: HIPAA grants patients rights over PHI with security requirements for counselors/agencies.

prompt: Medicare Advantage member grievance differs from coverage appeal because grievance involves:

options.text: Only Part A deductible amounts always
Criminal prosecution only
Changing Social Security number
Quality of care or plan service issues not strictly payment denial

explanation: Grievances address complaints; appeals address organization determinations/coverage.

prompt: Beneficiary designated representative may:

options.text: Act with documented authorization in appeals and communications
Sell beneficiary Medicare number online
Enroll beneficiary in any plan without consent always
Override beneficiary decision with no paperwork ever

explanation: Representative form allows helper/family member to assist with Medicare processes.

prompt: Provider balance billing in Original Medicare participating providers:

options.text: Always allowed unlimited
Generally prohibited from charging above Medicare allowed amount
Required by law always
Only applies to Part D drugs at pharmacy always

explanation: Participating providers accept assignment; balance billing limited with exceptions (non-participating, some services).

prompt: Beneficiary rights during hospital discharge include:

options.text: Immediate eviction without notice always
No right to receive medical record copy
Notice of discharge rights and expedited appeal of discharge if applicable
Forbidden from calling SHIP counselor

explanation: Important Message from Medicare and Detailed Notice of Discharge support appeal rights.

prompt: Medicare card protects beneficiary when counselor advises:

options.text: Post number on social media for convenience
Give to telemarketer promising free brace
Share with stranger at door for 'verification'
Never share Medicare number except to trusted providers/plans; treat like SSN

explanation: Medicare identity theft common; guard number and review MSN for fraud.

prompt: Limited English proficiency beneficiary should receive:

options.text: Meaningful language access such as interpreters and translated materials when available
English-only information without assistance always acceptable federally
Denied counseling
Higher Medicare premiums as penalty

explanation: Title VI and CMS guidance support language services for meaningful access.

prompt: Beneficiary advance directive counseling should clarify:

options.text: Advance directive automatically enrolls in Part C
Documents express care preferences; POLST/MOLST vary by state; not same as Medicare enrollment form
Provider must ignore always
Only attorney can have preferences

explanation: Counselors distinguish advance care planning documents from Medicare plan forms.

distractorExplanations.d: Individuals can create directives per state law.

prompt: Expedited appeal for termination of Medicare-covered services requires plan/provider to:

options.text: Stop all care immediately without notice always
Charge double during appeal automatically
Deliver standardized notice and continue services during appeal in qualifying situations
Ignore beneficiary request always

explanation: Expedited appeals have short timeframes; continuation of services may apply.

prompt: Beneficiary discrimination prohibited under Medicare-related laws includes:

options.text: Choice among lawful plan options
Neutral educational counseling
Explaining appeal deadlines factually
Race, color, national origin, disability, age in federally funded programs

explanation: Federal civil rights laws protect beneficiaries from discrimination in healthcare programs.

prompt: When beneficiary lacks capacity, counselor should:

options.text: Involve authorized representative/legal guardian per state law and document scope
Make all plan decisions alone without authority
Refuse all assistance permanently
Share PHI with unauthorized neighbor

explanation: Counseling respects legal decision-making authority and privacy rules.

prompt: Beneficiary right to unbiased information means counselor:

options.text: Must recommend highest commission plan
Does not sell insurance or receive marketing commissions from plans
Works for single MA plan exclusively as marketer
Hides Original Medicare option intentionally

explanation: SHIP and similar programs provide free neutral counseling without sales conflict.

prompt: Common Medicare fraud scheme involves:

options.text: Beneficiary reviewing MSN for accuracy
Counselor using CMS-approved materials
Billing for services not provided or not medically necessary
Provider documenting care appropriately

explanation: Fraud includes false claims, kickbacks, and identity theft for improper payment.

prompt: Beneficiary contacted 'Medicare will pay for back brace free' should:

options.text: Provide Medicare number and bank PIN immediately
Sign blank CMS forms
Assume all cold calls are CMS official always
Treat as potential scam; never give Medicare number; report suspicious contact

explanation: Durable medical equipment telemarketing scams steal identity and bill Medicare fraudulently.

prompt: Counselor learning beneficiary shared Medicare card with 'helper' who ordered supplies should:

options.text: Advise reporting to SMP, Medicare, and reviewing MSN for unauthorized charges
Ignore because free supplies nice
Encourage sharing number with more strangers
Tell beneficiary fraud impossible on Medicare

explanation: SMP (Senior Medicare Patrol) helps report fraud; unauthorized orders harm beneficiary and program.

distractorExplanations.b: Fraud harms program and beneficiary liability possible.

prompt: Kickback scheme in Medicare context is:

options.text: Legal discount fully compliant with AKS safe harbor when structured properly
Remuneration to induce referrals or use of services paid by federal programs
Counselor unpaid volunteer time
Beneficiary paying Medigap premium to insurer

explanation: Anti-Kickback Statute prohibits inducements for Medicare/Medicaid referrals.

prompt: Upcoding fraud occurs when provider:

options.text: Documents and codes accurately
Counselor explains benefits
Bills higher level service code than documented care supports
Beneficiary files legitimate appeal

explanation: Upcoding inflates reimbursement through false severity reporting.

prompt: Identity theft red flag on MSN shows:

options.text: Expected primary care visit locally
Correct hospitalization beneficiary recalls
Zero claims all year with no services received
Services in city beneficiary never visited during that period

explanation: Unknown services/locations on MSN suggest stolen Medicare number use.

distractorExplanations.c: No flag if truly no services.

prompt: Reporting Medicare fraud can include contacting:

options.text: 1-800-MEDICARE, SMP, HHS OIG hotline, or Medicare plan fraud line
Random email from unknown sender only
Social media influencer exclusively
Never report; keep secret always best

explanation: Multiple official channels investigate fraud; SMP provides local assistance.

prompt: Marketing violation during Medicare sales includes:

options.text: Scheduled appointment after beneficiary request
Door-to-door unsolicited contact prohibited in many MA/Part D marketing rules
Providing required plan materials at requested meeting
Compliant educational event with neutral CMS content

explanation: CMS marketing rules restrict unsolicited door-to-door and certain incentives.

prompt: Beneficiary asked to sign 'delivery receipt' for unrequested glucose monitor should:

options.text: Sign to be polite always accepting liability
Give Medicare card copy as tip
Refuse if not ordered; document; check MSN; report if billed
Install device without doctor order always covered

explanation: Unsolicited DME is common fraud; acceptance may imply consent to bill.

prompt: OIG exclusion list means excluded provider:

options.text: Gets bonus payments
Only excluded from dental
List optional for counselors to ignore
Cannot participate in federal healthcare programs; billing excluded provider prohibited

explanation: LEIE providers/entities cannot furnish services paid by Medicare/Medicaid.

prompt: Phishing email 'Verify Medicare account or lose benefits' best response:

options.text: Do not click links; contact Medicare via official phone on card/website
Enter Medicare number and bank info on link
Forward password to sender
Pay bitcoin to unlock account

explanation: CMS does not threaten loss via email links; phishing steals identity.

prompt: Counselor documenting suspected fraud should:

options.text: Accuse provider publicly without evidence
Record facts objectively and refer to SMP without investigating criminally themselves
Share beneficiary gossip widely
Destroy MSN evidence

explanation: Counselors support reporting with documentation; law enforcement investigates.

prompt: Medicaid/Medicare dual eligible fraud may involve:

options.text: Lawful coordination by ethical provider
Counselor explaining QMB benefits
Billing both programs improperly for same service
Beneficiary using MSP help correctly

explanation: Double billing across programs is fraud; proper billing follows COB rules.

prompt: Gift inducement during AEP such as cash for enrolling in MA plan is:

options.text: Required by Medicare
Unlimited if agent nice
Only illegal for Part A inpatient
Generally prohibited beyond nominal gifts per CMS marketing rules

explanation: CMS limits marketing gifts/inducements to prevent undue influence on plan choice.

distractorExplanations.c: Applies to MA/Part D marketing broadly.

prompt: Beneficiary recovery after fraud may include:

options.text: Disputing charges, changing Medicare number if compromised, and plan/provider investigation
Automatic full cash refund from counselor personally always
Ignoring unauthorized bills
Paying fraudulent charges to avoid trouble

explanation: Beneficiary should report, dispute, and protect identity; SMP/Medicare guide steps.

distractorExplanations.b: Counselor not liable for fraud restitution personally.

prompt: Neutral Medicare counseling session should begin with:

options.text: Signing enrollment form immediately
Needs assessment and confirming counselor is not selling plans
Collecting credit card for counselor fee from all callers always
Promising largest commission plan

explanation: SHIP-style counseling starts with scope, neutrality, and beneficiary-centered questions.

prompt: Motivational interviewing technique uses:

options.text: Interrupting beneficiary constantly
Telling beneficiary they are wrong about all concerns
Open questions, affirmations, reflective listening, and summarizing
Refusing to listen to preferences

explanation: MI supports behavior change and informed decision-making without pressure.

prompt: When beneficiary is overwhelmed, counselor should:

options.text: Dump all 100 pages at once without pause
End session abruptly without resources
Insist immediate decision on all programs today
Prioritize issues, break information into chunks, and schedule follow-up

explanation: Effective counseling paces information and confirms understanding (teach-back).

distractorExplanations.c: Decisions can take time within enrollment windows.

prompt: Teach-back method asks beneficiary to:

options.text: Explain in own words what they understood about next steps
Repeat counselor word-for-word only without understanding
Sign without reading
Skip questions to save time

explanation: Teach-back verifies comprehension of coverage choices and deadlines.

prompt: Counselor referral to State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP) is appropriate when:

options.text: Beneficiary wants illegal fraud tips
Beneficiary needs free unbiased help comparing Medicare options
Counselor sells Medigap and wants commission
Beneficiary needs veterinary advice

explanation: SHIP provides local Medicare counseling consistent with federal/state funding.

distractorExplanations.c: Sales conflict; refer SHIP for neutral help.

prompt: Documentation in counseling notes should:

options.text: Include gossip about beneficiary family for entertainment
Posted on public blog
Be factual, minimal necessary PHI, stored securely per agency policy
Omit all record of advice given

explanation: Confidential documentation supports continuity and grant compliance with privacy safeguards.

prompt: Beneficiary with employer coverage turning 65 should be counseled to:

options.text: Drop employer coverage immediately always without analysis
Ignore Special Enrollment Period rules
Enroll Part B without checking creditable coverage status
Compare employer plan vs Medicare timing, Part B delay rules, and spouse coverage

explanation: Active employment group coverage may allow Part B delay without penalty; individualized analysis needed.

distractorExplanations.a: Employer coverage may be better temporarily.

prompt: Low health literacy counseling strategy includes:

options.text: Plain language, visual aids, and avoiding jargon
Complex acronyms without explanation
Assuming beneficiary reads medical policy manuals for fun
Rushing through key cost-sharing terms

explanation: Plain language and visuals improve informed choice for all literacy levels.

prompt: Counselor boundaries when beneficiary offers expensive gift:

options.text: Accept car keys gladly always
Decline or follow agency policy on nominal gifts to avoid undue influence
Demand payment for all future calls secretly
Sell beneficiary insurance on side

explanation: Professional boundaries maintain trust and program integrity.

prompt: Cultural humility in counseling means:

options.text: Stereotyping groups uniformly
Ignoring beneficiary values
Recognizing counselor does not know all cultural contexts and asking respectful questions
Assuming Medicare rules differ by ethnicity illegally

explanation: Humility supports respectful individualized counseling within same Medicare rules.

prompt: Follow-up plan after complex LIS and MSP application counseling should include:

options.text: No written summary ever
Promise guaranteed approval
Tell beneficiary never call SSA/Medicaid offices
Checklist of documents, agency contacts, deadlines, and scheduled follow-up call

explanation: Action plans with contacts reduce missed deadlines for assistance programs.

prompt: Counselor faced with angry beneficiary about denied claim should:

options.text: Listen, validate emotion, explain appeal path, and offer practical next steps
Hang up immediately without explanation
Yell back to assert dominance
Guarantee win on appeal falsely

explanation: De-escalation with accurate appeal information supports beneficiary empowerment.

prompt: Screening for Medicare Savings Programs (MSP) includes asking about:

options.text: Favorite movie only
Income and resources relative to state thresholds for QMB/SLMB/QI
Neighbor's income only
Stock tips

explanation: MSP help with Part B premiums/cost-sharing based on income/resource limits varying by state.

prompt: Group presentation vs individual counseling difference:

options.text: Group must enroll all in same plan
Individual sessions may ignore beneficiary questions
Group educates generally; individual addresses personal eligibility and choices confidentially
Group allows sharing PHI of attendees publicly

explanation: Education events complement but do not replace personalized counseling.

prompt: Closing counseling session effectively includes:

options.text: Leave beneficiary confused intentionally
Pressure signature on unknown form
Delete beneficiary phone number
Summarize decisions/next steps, provide written resources, and confirm contact for questions

explanation: Closure reinforces understanding and accessible follow-up within program rules.

distractorExplanations.c: Contact info needed for follow-up if permitted.
